title: Serialize user-visible diff handoff before merge
goal: Prevent parallel actors from merging a nontrivial pull request before the exact head- and diff-bound artifact has been exposed to the user, while preserving fail-closed head-drift handling and avoiding duplicate merge effects.

acceptance.assertion: A nontrivial merge is authorized only by a durable delivery receipt proving that the complete downloadable diff artifact was exposed to the user before merge dispatch; artifact creation alone is insufficient.

The delivery receipt, critical review and merge request bind the same repository, pull request, exact head SHA and complete GitHub diff SHA-256; any head change invalidates all three and requires regeneration and redelivery.
A merge-intent resource or equivalent atomic gate prevents another cooperating Grabowski actor from merging the same pull request while artifact delivery or review is pending.

If GitHub shows that another actor already merged, the operation performs no duplicate effect, records whether the pre-merge delivery contract was satisfied and never treats post-merge exposure as equivalent evidence.
Receipts expose artifact creation time, user-visible delivery time, merge-dispatch time and GitHub merge time in a comparable clock domain, with ordering uncertainty stated explicitly.
Tests cover two concurrent merge actors, head drift after delivery, delivery failure, already-merged reconciliation, stale receipts and an exact successful artifact-review-delivery-merge sequence.
The gate grants no merge authority by itself and cannot replace task authorization, CI, critical review, recovery, branch protection or human authorization requirements.

metadata.source: live process race during Grabowski PR #181 closeout

observation.finding: A parallel actor merged the exact reviewed head before this thread exposed the downloadable artifact link to the user. The code and deployment were valid, but the required user-visible ordering was not serialized.

metadata.risk: Process evidence can appear complete after the fact even though the user did not receive the artifact before the irreversible merge effect.
